items: June payrolls were the major scheduled catalyst before the holiday weekend.
The prior session had exposed a sharp split between positive breadth and weak AI/chip leadership.
Investors were balancing softer growth data against the risk of further semiconductor profit-taking.

intraday_and_postclose_catalysts: Payroll growth came in much weaker than expected, reducing expectations for a near-term Fed hike.
Dow and broad-market shares benefited from lower-rate expectations.
Semiconductors sold off sharply for a second day, leaving the S&P 500 flat and Nasdaq lower despite positive breadth.
causal_narrative: The macro impulse was dovish, but the index response was fragmented. Rate-sensitive and non-tech shares rallied on weak payroll growth, while crowded AI/chip positions continued to unwind. SPY therefore behaved as a tug-of-war between broad easing-sensitive strength and concentrated technology weakness.
alternative_explanations: Options and dealer positioning may amplify or oppose the public-news impulse.
Index concentration can make SPY and SPX diverge from equal-weight breadth.
